name: Apple Oat Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk, sour milk, or plain yogurt (thinned to the consistency of buttermilk)
1 Egg or 2 Egg Whites or 1/4 c Egg Substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray muffin pan with non-stick cooking spray.
2. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the buttermilk, oats, and raisins until combined.
3. In a separate bowl, blend together the whole wheat flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt.
4. Vigorously beat the egg and honey in a separate container.
5. Gradually fold the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients just until mixed. Do not overmix!
6. Gently fold in the grated apple.
7. Fill each muffin cup about 2/3 full with batter. Fill any remaining cups about half full with water.
8. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes.
9. Allow muffins to cool in the pan for 5 to 10 minutes before removing them. Gently tug on the side of each muffin to loosen, then turn muffins upside down when ready to serve.
Best served warm.
Variation: Omit oats and use 1 3/4 cups whole wheat pastry flour instead.
